en: This requirement is triggered because you indicated your organization maintains valid Philippines Statutory Labor Insurance documentation in the Philippines Statutory Labor Insurance Applicability Assessment questionnaire.

A Private Sector Company is defined as any individual or entity (company, partnership, cooperative, non-profit, etc.) that employs at least one worker and is not part of the government sector.\n\nA Government Sector Operation covers most government employees except uniformed personnel, certain casual/contractual staff, and those covered by special laws or on honoraria only.
The SSS (Social Security System) remittance receipt or electronic contribution report can be accessed these through the SSS online portal.

Please upload your company's valid SSS (Social Security System) remittance receipt or electronic contribution report to confirm your company's registration Philippines’ Employees’ Compensation (EC) Program social insurance requirement.\n
The GSIS (Government Service Insurance System) Contribution Certificate can be accessed via your local GSIS office or on your online eGSISMO portal.\n

Please upload your valid GSIS (Government Service Insurance System) Contribution Certificate to confirm your company's registration for Philippines’ Employees’ Compensation (EC) Program social insurance requirement.

Your Certificiation/Attestation Letter on company letterhead signed by a senior HR or compliance officer should affirm the following:

- The EC Logbook is duly maintained according to legal requirements.

- All entries are up-to-date and available for government inspection.

- No employee PII is disclosed in the compliance confirmation provided to external third parties.

Please upload a  valid Certification/Attestation Letter on company letterhead signed by a senior HR or compliance officer to confirm your company's registration for Philippines’ Employees’ Compensation (EC) Program social insurance requirement.
